Tier-1 Off-Grid ← Dark 30 App Page
Documentation / Operator Manual

Tier 1 Dark 30 — Operator Manual

July 2026

SECTION 1 — WHAT THIS APP IS

Tier 1 Dark 30 is a survival and preparedness training app built for sustained grid-down scenarios. It is not a reference wiki, not a gear catalog, and not a beginner camping guide. Every module is written to operator standard — meaning it assumes the situation is real, resources are limited, and someone is depending on you to know what you are doing.

The app works fully offline. No internet connection is required for any feature. No account is required. Nothing is transmitted. Everything lives on your device.

SECTION 2 — NAVIGATION OVERVIEW

The home screen has 7 sections. Tap any tile to enter that section.

Section What It Does Start Here If
Situation 8-question assessment engine that generates a prioritized action plan You don't know where to start
Scenarios 83 operational playbooks across 8 threat categories You need a full picture of a specific threat
Skills 114 training modules across 14 categories You need to learn or reference a specific skill
Mindset 7 modules on operator psychology and decision-making You need to understand how to think under pressure
Tools Live calculators and planners You need to calculate water needs, plan comms, build a loadout
Reference 21 quick-access field reference cards You need fast information on a specific topic
My Plan Personalized operator profile (coming soon) Post-launch feature

SECTION 3 — FREE VS PREMIUM CONTENT

The app has a free tier and a paid tier. One-time purchase of $24.99 unlocks everything permanently.

What's Free What's Paid ($24.99 one time)
First 2 skill modules per category All remaining skill modules (100+)
First scenario per threat category All remaining scenario modules (75+)
All 21 reference cards Unlimited Situation Assessments
Signal Suite (fully free) All tools — Water/Food Calculator, Comms Planner, Gear Loadout Builder, Compass
3 Situation Assessments Operator Briefing screen
Mindset modules

To unlock: tap any locked item and follow the prompt. Or go to Settings and tap Unlock Full Access.

SECTION 4 — SITUATION ASSESSMENT ENGINE

The Situation Assessment Engine is the fastest path to a prioritized action plan when you don't know where to start.

How to use it

  1. Tap Situation on the home screen
  2. Answer 8 questions — threat type, location, group composition, supply status, infrastructure status, immediate danger level, medical status, and time elapsed since the event
  3. Review your result — threat level (Green / Yellow / Orange / Red), immediate actions, 72-hour priorities, and linked modules relevant to your situation
  4. Tap any linked module to go directly to relevant content
  5. Tap Re-Assess at any time to run a new assessment as your situation changes

Free users get 3 assessments. Premium users get unlimited.

SECTION 5 — SCENARIOS

83 scenario modules organized into 8 threat categories:

  • Grid and Infrastructure Collapse (8 modules)
  • Civil Unrest (10 modules)
  • Government Breakdown (10 modules)
  • Invasion and Occupation (10 modules)
  • Nuclear and Radiological (12 modules)
  • Biological and Pandemic (11 modules)
  • Chemical and EMP (11 modules)
  • Natural Disaster (11 modules)

How to navigate

  1. Tap Scenarios on the home screen
  2. Select a threat category
  3. Select a specific scenario
  4. Read the full module — immediate actions, 72-hour plan, extended strategy, community response

Each module is a complete operational playbook. Read the ones relevant to your threat environment before you need them. The time to read a nuclear scenario playbook is not after the flash.

SECTION 6 — SKILLS

114 skill modules across 14 categories. Each module is written to operator standard with standard operating procedures, time benchmarks, degraded condition notes, and an Operator Notes section covering the most critical insight for that skill.

Categories

Fire 14 MODULES

Bow drill, hand drill, flint and steel, battery and steel wool, magnification, chemical methods, wet conditions, fire lays, signal fire, cooking fire, warmth fire, fire safety, banking, and more.

Shelter 14 MODULES

Debris hut, lean-to, A-frame, snow shelter, urban shelter, vehicle shelter, tarp and poncho configurations, underground, insulation, raised platform, desert, and swamp shelter.

Water 15 MODULES

Terrain sourcing, urban sourcing, solar still, natural and urban filtration, boiling, collection, chemical treatment, plant sources, storage, dehydration recognition, and more.

Medical and Trauma 12 MODULES

Hemorrhage control, airway management, chest wounds, fractures, burns, hypothermia, infection, heat illness, TCCC, MARCH protocol, dental emergencies, survival pharmacy.

Navigation 10 MODULES

Compass, map reading, celestial navigation, terrain association, urban navigation, route planning, dead reckoning, water navigation, grid coordinates, night navigation.

Food 7 MODULES

Fishing, cooking, trapping, nutrition and caloric needs, foraging basics.

Communications 6 MODULES

Radio fundamentals, COMSEC and signal discipline, visual signaling, Meshtastic mesh networking, net operations, runner systems.

Security and Home Defense 5 MODULES

Home defense, access control and checkpoints, watch rotation, reaction to contact, immediate action drills.

Intelligence 4 MODULES

Area assessment, pattern of life, neighborhood intelligence network, threat analysis.

Movement and Patrol 6 MODULES

Patrol fundamentals, urban movement, cover and concealment, evasion and escape, vehicle operations, advanced patrol.

Primitive Weapons and Defense 9 MODULES

Bow and arrow, sling, spear, knapping, club, traps, edged weapons, atlatl, staff.

Improvised Tools 4 MODULES

Basic cordage, primitive pottery, primitive metalworking, advanced rope making.

Situational Awareness 4 MODULES

Color code system, threat assessment, pattern of life analysis, counter-surveillance.

Community Organization 4 MODULES

Community structure and governance, barter and trade economy, community resistance, food production at scale.

How to use

  1. Tap Skills on the home screen
  2. Select a category
  3. Select a module
  4. Read the full content — use the font size toggle (Aa) to adjust for your conditions
  5. Use PDF export to save a module for offline reference outside the app

SECTION 7 — SIGNAL SUITE

The Signal Suite is a fully functional communications toolkit accessible from within the Skills section under Communications. Five tabs.

FLASHER

Type any message. The app converts it to Morse code and flashes your phone LED in the correct dot-dash sequence. Adjust speed with the WPM slider. Use this to signal aircraft, distant observers, or any party who can see your phone.

DECODER

Tap the large button to input Morse manually. Short tap equals dot. Long tap equals dash. The app detects letter gaps and word gaps automatically and displays the decoded text in real time.

SOUND SIGNALS

Plays standard survival sound signal sequences. Three blasts for distress. Two blasts for all clear. One blast for attention. SOS in Morse by audio. Each has a play button and displays the international standard meaning.

VISUAL REFERENCE

Complete ground-to-air signal reference. International symbols (V, X, arrow, LL, F), body signals to aircraft, panel signal colors and placement, day and night signaling methods, mirror flash technique.

SIGNAL DISCIPLINE

Full COMSEC and communications security guide covering brevity codes, authentication, duress signals, frequency discipline, and transmission minimization.

SECTION 8 — TOOLS

Water and Food Calculator

Water Calculator

Input number of people, days, activity level, climate, and special conditions. Outputs drinking water, sanitation water, and cooking water totals in liters and gallons, plus container counts and purification tablet requirements. Updates live as you change inputs.

Food Calculator

Input people, days, activity level, and age group factors. Outputs total calories, macros, practical storage weight, and conversion to common stored food quantities.

Storage Planner

Combined view showing total water volume, food weight, storage space needed, container manifest, rotation schedule, and acquisition priority order.

Reference

Water purification comparison, food shelf life table, caloric density reference, dehydration signs, starvation timeline.

Communications Planner

Frequency Planner

Build your PACE frequency plan. Select radio type, assign channels, set CTCSS tones, tag each channel with its use case. Output is a formatted PACE card designed to be screenshot.

Net Schedule Builder

Input your net name, NCS callsign, stations, and check-in frequency. Generates a full 24-hour net schedule and a net log template.

Range Estimator

Input radio type, power output, antenna type, and terrain. Outputs estimated ground-to-ground and ground-to-elevated range with a schematic range diagram.

Meshtastic Planner

Input node count, node types, terrain, and target coverage area. Outputs coverage analysis, gap analysis, repeater placement recommendations, and hardware cost estimate.

Reference

US radio band quick reference, GMRS channel chart, HAM band plan, phonetic alphabet, COMSEC checklist.

Gear Loadout Builder

Build

Three expandable layers — EDC (Every Day Carry), INCH Bag, Vehicle/Base Kit. 79 pre-populated items across all three layers. Check items you have. Add custom items. Enter weights.

Weight Analysis

Live weight tracking per layer. Carry percentage vs body weight. Category breakdown. Missing category flags.

Readiness Score

Scored across 8 categories — Medical, Water, Fire/Shelter, Navigation, Communications, Security, Food, Documentation. Overall Tier rating from Tier 1 Operator down to Unprepared.

Resupply Tracker

Track consumable expiration dates. Color-coded alerts — red for rotate now, yellow for rotate soon, green for good.

Reference

Common gear failures, weight reduction strategies, EDC philosophy, gear quality tiers by category.

Offline Compass

Analog compass rose with bearing lock, True North and Magnetic North toggle, back bearing calculation, GPS position overlay, tactical mode, declination adjustment, and calibration screen.

Power and Electrical Reference

Seven tabs: Power Calculator, Ohm's Law, Wire Sizing guide, Voltage Reference, Electrical Formulas, Fuse Guide, Battery Comparison.

SECTION 9 — REFERENCE CARDS

21 quick-access reference cards. Each card is a fast-read formatted reference on a single critical topic.

  • Bug-out checklist
  • Chemical agent symptoms
  • Communications frequencies
  • KI dosing guide
  • Medical vitals reference
  • Meshtastic quickstart
  • Navigation quick reference
  • Nuclear shelter protection factors
  • Power quick reference
  • Radiation dose reference
  • Security assessment checklist
  • Signals reference
  • Trauma first aid
  • Water treatment methods
  • Weather reading
  • Fire starting reference
  • Plus 5 additional cards

How to use

  1. Tap Reference on the home screen
  2. Tap any card
  3. Use the screenshot function on your phone to save cards you may need in the field

These cards work without the app. Screenshot them before you need them.

SECTION 10 — SETTINGS

Access Settings via the gear icon in the top right of the home screen.

  • Display mode toggle — Light / Dark / Tactical Black
  • Font size preference
  • Restore Purchases — tap to restore a previous purchase on a new device
  • Premium status display
  • Reviewer unlock — tap the version number 7 times to unlock all content (for App Store review only)
  • App version and copyright information

SECTION 11 — OFFLINE USE

This app is built to work with zero network connectivity.

What works offline: Everything. All 225+ modules, all tools, all reference cards, all calculators, all planners, the compass, the signal suite. Everything.

What requires a connection: Nothing in v1.0 except the optional topographic map tile download (coming in a future update). That download is user-initiated. Nothing else phones home.

To prepare for field use

  1. Download the app while connected
  2. Complete the purchase while connected
  3. After that — airplane mode works permanently

SECTION 12 — TROUBLESHOOTING

Purchase not showing as unlocked after buying

Go to Settings and tap Restore Purchases. This re-validates your purchase with the App Store and restores premium access.

App showing locked content after reinstall

Tap Restore Purchases in Settings. Your purchase is tied to your Apple ID or Google account — it is not lost when you reinstall.

Compass not showing accurate bearing

Calibrate the compass — go to the compass screen, tap the calibration icon, and follow the figure-8 motion instructions. Keep the phone away from metal objects and magnets.

Flashlight not working in Signal Suite

The Morse flasher requires camera permission to control the LED. Go to your phone Settings and grant camera permission to Tier 1 Dark 30.

SECTION 13 — CONTACT AND SUPPORT

For support, questions, or to report an issue:

Email: tier1offgrid@protonmail.com

Support page: tier1offgrid.com/dist/support.html

This app is built and maintained by one person. Response times may vary. For urgent issues, Substack is the fastest path.

TIER-1 OFF-GRID

Built for what's coming.
